title: For Iran, Flexing Control Over Waterway Is New Deterrent
summary: Iran’s government could emerge from the conflict with a blueprint to keep adversaries at bay, regardless of any restrictions on its nuclear program.

title: ‘Turning Point Was Charlie Kirk’: Why This Student Group Moved On
summary: Students at the University of Arkansas disagreed with Turning Point’s direction, pointing to challenges ahead for the conservative group.

title: The Primary That Could End a State G.O.P.’s Independent Streak
summary: For years, Republican state legislators in Montana have been willing to team up with Democrats, but in nearly two dozen races on June 2, a nationally attuned right has those lawmakers in its sights.

title: The Doctor Will Seek Your Vote Now
summary: Dozens of Democratic doctors are running for office in the midterms, including some spurred by opposition to Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and his anti-vaccine stance.

title: Maduro’s Successor Is Purging Allies Who Kept Him in Power in Venezuela
summary: The successor to Venezuela’s captured President Nicolás Maduro is purging the people who kept him in power.

title: New York’s Anti-Rich Current Reaches Crescendo With Second-Home Tax Plan
summary: With a new tax proposal, the threat of a building workers’ strike and Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s shunning of the Met Gala, the city’s wealth gap was on full display.

title: Contours of NYC’s Pied-à-Terre Tax Begin to Take Shape
summary: As state leaders determine how much owners of high-priced second homes in New York City may have to pay, they are also wrestling with opaque L.L.C.-ownership issues.

title: Activists Urge Boycott of ‘Bezos Met Gala’ With Posters Around NYC
summary: A guerrilla activist group is covering New York with posters criticizing the billionaire Jeff Bezos’ involvement in the event, a fund-raiser for the Metropolitan Museum.

summary: A higher authority finally tells Trump he’s no messiah.

summary: The president loves the 19th century. But why?

title: Trading Spaces
summary: With spring comes the urge to spring-clean. It’s about so much more than just organizing your closet.

title: Pope Says News Outlets Misread Some of His Remarks as Criticism of Trump
summary: Pope Leo responded directly on Monday to a presidential attack. But since then, he said, some of his statements during his Africa trip have been misconstrued.

title: Who Is the New Leader of Hungary?
summary: Our reporter Andrew Higgins describes why Hungary’s voters chose Peter Magyar over Viktor Orban in a landslide, ending Orban’s 16 years in power.

title: After D4vd Arrest, Lake Elsinore Residents Mourn Celeste Rivas Hernandez
summary: Celeste Rivas Hernandez went missing from Lake Elsinore, Calif., at age 13. The musician known as D4vd has been detained in connection with her death.

title: New PEPFAR Data Show Worrying Declines in Testing and Treatment for H.I.V.
summary: The numbers are the first to quantify the effect of the Trump administration’s shutdown and restarting of a program that has saved millions of lives worldwide.

title: Migrant Children Removed From New York Shelter After Abuse Allegations
summary: Federal officials received complaints about the treatment of children and adolescents at a facility in Westchester County, N.Y.
